title: Dhodap Fort
tagline: Dhodap Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Dhodap is one of the hill forts in Maharashtra state in India.
overview.description: Dhodap is one of the hill forts in Maharashtra state in India. Situated in Chandwad taluka in the Nashik district. Base village is Hatti. the fort is 4829 ft (1472 mt) above sea level.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Abhona
topPlaces.description: Abhona is a village in Kalwan tehsil of Nashik district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It belongs to the North Maharashtra region.

topPlaces.name: Chankapur Dam
topPlaces.description: Chankapur Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Girna River near Abhona in Kalwan tehsil of Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India. The dam were built in 19th century by British.

topPlaces.name: Nanduri Dam
topPlaces.description: Nanduri Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Tapti river near Ama Local Nallaher, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Ahivant Fort
topPlaces.description: Ahivant Fort is a fort located 55km from Nashik, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra. This fort is an important fort in Nashik district.

topPlaces.name: Satmala Range
topPlaces.description: Satmala (pronunciation:[sɐtmaːla]) is a mountain range which runs across Nashik District, Maharashtra. They are an integral part of the Sahyadris range within Nashik.

topPlaces.name: Saptashrungi
topPlaces.description: Saptashrungi or Saptashringi (Marathi: सप्तशृङ्गि, Saptaśr̥ṇgī) is a site of Hindu pilgrimage in the village of Vani, situated 60 kilometres (37 mi) from Nashik in Indian state of Maharashtra. According to Hindu traditions, the goddess Saptashrungi Nivasini dwells within the seven mountain peaks.

items.note: Abhona is a village in Kalwan tehsil of Nashik district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It belongs to the North Maharashtra region.
Chankapur Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Girna River near Abhona in Kalwan tehsil of Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India. The dam were built in 19th century by British.
Nanduri Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Tapti river near Ama Local Nallaher, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.
Ahivant Fort is a fort located 55km from Nashik, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra. This fort is an important fort in Nashik district.
Satmala (pronunciation:[sɐtmaːla]) is a mountain range which runs across Nashik District, Maharashtra. They are an integral part of the Sahyadris range within Nashik.

Saptashrungi or Saptashringi (Marathi: सप्तशृङ्गि, Saptaśr̥ṇgī) is a site of Hindu pilgrimage in the village of Vani, situated 60 kilometres (37 mi) from Nashik in Indian state of Maharashtra. According to Hindu traditions, the goddess Saptashrungi Nivasini dwells within the seven mountain peaks.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dhodap Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dhodap Fort is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dhodap Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (100 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (59 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dhodap Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dhodap Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dhodap Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Dhodap Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dhodap Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Abhona, Chankapur Dam, Nanduri Dam, Ahivant Fort, Satmala Range.
